# Reproducer derived from https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues/23159
# repo: ray-project/ray
# title: [Train] Print actionable error message when CUDA object returned from training function

# Last frames in traceback:
#   /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/site-packages/torch/serialization.py line 713 in load

#   /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/site-packages/torch/serialization.py line 876 in persistent_load
#   /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/site-packages/torch/serialization.py line 176 in default_restore_location
#   /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/site-packages/torch/serialization.py line 152 in _cuda_deserialize
#   /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/site-packages/torch/serialization.py line 136 in validate_cuda_device

# Minimal repro (conceptual):

def repro():
    # Conditions described in the issue:
    # ### Search before asking
    # - [X] I had searched in the [issues](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues) and found no similar feature requirement
    # ### Description
    # Provide a clear and actionable error message when the user returns an object on CUDA from the training function
    # ### Use case
    raise RuntimeError('reproduce: see https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues/23159')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    repro()
